Discord Role Bot Tutorial: Auto-Assign Roles via Invite Links (Invite-Role Bot)
The company has been gradually moving onto Discord, and there are a few features Discord doesn't have natively. So here's a quick post about one of them.
The author of this bot doesn't seem very responsive these days. If you'd rather use something more actively maintained,
Normally, when you want to invite a friend to your Discord server, you go through "Create Invite" (next to the gear icon on a channel):
If you don't tweak the settings, the link will expire after 7 days. You can change that by clicking "Edit invite link" below:

Heads up: invite links are tied to the channel you generated them from. If you generate one in a private channel, anyone joining via that link enters the private channel — which usually causes weird issues (like the user seeing a blank screen until they switch channels or restart the Discord client). The safer bet is to always generate invites from a public channel.
Inviting Invite-Role Bot to your server
The typical scenario: you want members joining via a specific link to automatically get role X. Discord doesn't ship with this, so you need a third-party bot. The one we'll use here is
First, invite the bot to your Discord server:
Pick the server you want it in:
Done — the bot is now in your server:
Now we'll create a test role. First, go to Server Settings:
Configure the role however you need — this is just a demo, so I'll create something minimal:
Heads up: Invite-Role Bot only handles role names that start with an English letter. So make sure your role name begins with English.
Wiring the invite link to a role
This is the main event. Quick rundown of Invite-Role Bot's commands:
: Show configured invite-link → role mappings. Often contains sensitive info, so use in a private channel. Needs admin permission.
: Map an invite link to a role. Anyone joining via that link gets the role automatically.
: Get the bot invite link to share with others.
The commands you'll actually use are
We already covered how to make an invite link, so I won't repeat that. In a channel, type:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
to actually select the role — typing the name as plain text won't work.)
If it worked, you'll see a success message:
Now share that invite link. Anyone joining via it will get your configured role automatically.
keeps failing, the issue is probably that the bot's own role is too low in the hierarchy. Just drag it up.
A bot can only assign roles that are
in the hierarchy. Pull the bot's role up and it'll work.

When you want new members to automatically get a specific role based on which invite link they used, vanilla Discord can't do it on its own — you need a bot. This post walks through using Invite-Role Bot to wire invite links to roles.
